  3. How to become a referee

How to become a referee

This is a plain-English guide. The official IPF Technical Rules are the final word.

There are two levels of IPF international referee: Category 2, and the higher Category 1. (To referee at our competitions, you take the divisional referees exam first, see our FAQs.)

Category 2 referee

  • Be a national referee of at least two years, having judged at least two national championships
  • Be recommended by your national federation
  • Pass a written and a practical exam, scoring at least 85% on each

For the practical, you judge 100 attempts as a side referee, at least 40 of them squats, and must agree with the scrutineers at least 85% of the time.

Category 1 referee

  • Be a Category 2 referee for at least four years, having judged at least six international and six national championships
  • Pass a written and practical exam, judging at least 75 attempts as chief referee, scoring at least 90% overall
  • Be nominated by your national federation at least three months before

Category 1 referees must speak English well enough to handle disputes and jury duty.

Dress code

Referees wear a dark blue blazer with the IPF badge, grey trousers or skirt, a white shirt or blouse, and the IPF tie or scarf, red for Category 1 and blue for Category 2, with formal black shoes.

Re-registration

Referees re-register every four years, having judged at least two international and two national championships in that time, with a small fee. A referee inactive for four years loses their qualification and must re-sit the exams.

Next: the jury
